title: oxalis uliginosa
intro: Oxalis uliginosa, also known as marsh-wood sorrel or marsh sorrel, is a perennial plant species in the wood sorrel family (Oxalidaceae) that is native to Europe and Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and act as an antiseptic. The plant's leaves and flowers are rich in antioxidants, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds that offer health benefits such as reducing oxidative stress, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems, skin conditions, and respiratory issues.
